meta.title: Seedance 2 API | APIDot
meta.description: Access ByteDance Seedance 2 instantly on APIDot with text, image, video, and audio inputs, native audio-video generation, and up to 1080p output.

hero.title: Seedance 2 API Playground
hero.description: Test ByteDance Seedance 2 and seedance-2-fast instantly. Use text, image, video, and audio references with native audio-video generation, multi-shot storytelling, and director-level camera control, then move the same setup into APIDot's async production workflow.

overview.title: Why teams choose {modelName}
paragraphs: {modelName} is ByteDance's multimodal video model for production-grade generation. It combines text, image, video, and audio inputs in one workflow, with native audio-video synthesis, multi-shot storytelling, stronger character consistency, and cinematic control over motion, lighting, and pacing.

On APIDot, call the active variant with model="{activeModelId}". {activeModelName} uses the same async submit, polling, and webhook lifecycle as the rest of the platform, so validated playground settings can move directly into production.

items.title: Native audio-video joint generation
items.description: Generate visuals, dialogue, ambience, and timing cues in a single pass, reducing the need for separate lip-sync and post-production audio alignment.

items.title: Multimodal references with stronger consistency
items.description: Combine text, image, video, and audio references to lock identity, product appearance, visual style, motion language, and rhythm for complex creative briefs.

items.title: Multi-shot storytelling and continuity
items.description: Produce multi-shot clips from one request while keeping characters, style, and narrative continuity stable across cuts and transitions.

items.title: Standard and Fast variants
items.description: Use the standard model for higher-end delivery and 1080p output, or switch to the Fast variant for lower-cost iteration while keeping the same APIDot workflow.

useCases.title: What can you build with {modelName}?
useCases.description: Teams typically use {activeModelName} in the following production scenarios:
items: Advertising concepts, product films, and social-first short video
Film pre-visualization, shot planning, and storyboard validation
Multilingual localization with native lip-sync variants
Game cutscenes and interactive media sequences
Training content with consistent presenters and narration
Music videos, dance clips, and cinematic b-roll

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.description: Generate an API key in your dashboard and get immediate access to Seedance 2 through APIDot without waitlists or manual approval.

steps.title: Top up credits
steps.description: Billing is usage-based per second. Add credits based on the model, resolution, and whether your request includes video input.

steps.title: Integrate the async workflow
steps.description: Submit model="{activeModelId}" requests to {endpoint}, then poll status or consume webhook callbacks to retrieve the final media output in your app.

items.question: What is {modelName}?
items.answer: {modelName} is ByteDance's multimodal video generation model. It supports text, image, video, and audio inputs with native audio-video generation, director-level control, stronger character consistency, and multi-shot storytelling.

items.question: What inputs does {modelName} support?
items.answer: In APIDot's Seedance 2 workflow, you can start with a text prompt and optionally attach image, video, and audio references. The playground supports first/last frames, reference images, reference video, and reference audio.

items.question: What are the duration and resolution limits?
items.answer: This page supports 4 to 15 second generations. seedance-2 supports 480p, 720p, and 1080p, while seedance-2-fast supports 480p and 720p. 1080p is available on seedance-2 only.

items.question: Does {modelName} support native audio and lip-sync?
items.answer: Yes. Native audio-video generation is one of Seedance 2's core strengths, letting the model produce dialogue, ambience, rhythm, and lip-sync together during generation.

items.question: How is it different from Seedance 1.0 or 1.5?
items.answer: Seedance 2 pushes further on multimodal references, native audio, continuity across shots, and director-level control. It is better suited for complex commercial, narrative, and reference-driven video workflows.

items.question: Is {modelName} suitable for commercial or production use?
items.answer: Yes. APIDot exposes Seedance 2 through a production-ready async API with usage-based billing and a workflow that fits creative tools, automation systems, and commercial content pipelines. Commercial rights still depend on your platform terms.

items.question: How quickly can I start using {modelName}?
items.answer: Usually within minutes. Create an API key, top up credits, submit a request with model="{activeModelId}", and retrieve results through the async APIDot flow.
